Example team card: Attendence is calculated from Club size, how far the away team needs to travel and Weather condition
Attendence doesn't affect gameplay in any way, it is a cosmetic/immersing feature.

column9: Coaches normally only changes between seasons. - see table in sheet "Changes between seasons"
A random chance at the end of each season, and if the coach didn't change the last season, the next season there is a higher chance of change.
A coach can have both the plus 1 attack positive trait, and the minus 1 attack negative trait, resulting in a net zero.
This to keep it as easy as possible, but also to not reduce the amount of combinations.
Another bonus could be the narritive regarding some coaches and the strange combinations.

Example team card: If it is a derby, the away crowd is trippled in size
Then depending on the away team Club size and how far they need to travel a number is added to the base. The longer the travel the lower the number.
2 cells in any direction from team location
5 cells in any direction from team location
9 cells in any direction from team location
14 cells in any direction from team location

column5: Adds 10% to both home and away crowd

Subtracts 50% to both home and away crowd

Example team card: Then 2 die rolls are simulated, in order to have some randomness to the crowd figures.
Roll 1 - determins if the second die will add or subtract attendence - 50% chance of adding or 50% to subtracting attendence
Roll 2 - determins the percentage that the attendence will change with. Random 1-30 for how much it goes up or down.
Team A plays at home, Team B has to travel 7 cells.
Base for team a is 25 000 (club size 7)
Away crowd for team B is 1 000 (club size 4)
Team B has to travel 7 cells, which means 40% away crowd will come, 400, (40% out of 1000)
It is sunny weather which adds 10% to both home and away crowds
So home crowd is 27 500 (25000 *1.1) and away crowd is 440 (400*1,1)
Base for the match is now 27 940
The first die roll is Odd, which means the next roll will subtract attendence
The second roll is 11, which means the attendence will be 11% lower.
The final attendence for the game is 24 867 (27 940 * 0.89)
Experimenting - max attendence, two teams, club size 9, derby, and a sunny day, coach attribute

Random rolls add and roll 30 for max result.
